scenario: An IDP Application for invoices was trained on 100 samples. The model occasionally misextracts vendor names. The team wants to improve the model by labelling new samples.
question: Which IDP workflow fits?

options.text: Iterate on the IDP Application's extraction template in Web Modeler -- upload more representative sample documents covering layout variation, refine each extraction field's Prompt to better describe the target value, pick a stronger LLM extraction model if accuracy plateaus, and validate via the Validate extraction tab plus saved test cases. Camunda IDP uses LLM-driven extraction with in-context learning from prompts and field definitions -- there is no fine-tuning of model weights in this process.

The IDP workflow explicitly supports iterative improvement through prompt refinement, additional sample documents, LLM model selection, and test-case-based validation without requiring a fresh application from scratch. Teams building on Camunda 8 should consider this when designing process models for production deployment. This reflects the Zeebe engine's design philosophy.

Editing FEEL expressions operates at the BPMN process orchestration layer, which executes after IDP extraction has already produced its output. FEEL cannot influence how the LLM extracts vendor names from document images. The extraction accuracy problem must be addressed within the IDP Application's prompt and sample configuration, not downstream in FEEL. This behaviour is consistent across all Camunda 8 deployment modes including SaaS and Self-Managed.

Bypassing IDP entirely and using a custom Connector eliminates all the built-in extraction workflow, UI integration, test-case management, and LLM model selection that Camunda IDP provides. Building a custom solution from scratch requires significantly more development effort and loses the purpose-built document intelligence tooling.

a.text: Correct. IDP Applications support iterative improvement via prompt + sample refinement (NOT model fine-tuning). Workflow: upload more sample documents covering layout variation, sharpen each field's Prompt text, optionally switch the underlying LLM extraction model, save test cases on the Validate tab to compare runs, then republish a new template version (template versioning is via Web Modeler version mechanism per idp-unstructured-extraction.md).

b.text: Incorrect. No need for fresh app.

c.text: Incorrect. FEEL doesn't change extraction model accuracy.

d.text: Incorrect. Custom Connector replaces IDP's whole offering.

explanation: IDP Applications support iterative improvement via prompt + sample refinement (NOT model fine-tuning). Workflow: upload more sample documents covering layout variation, sharpen each field's Prompt text, optionally switch the underlying LLM extraction model, save test cases on the Validate tab to compare runs, then republish a new template version (template versioning is via Web Modeler version mechanism per idp-unstructured-extraction.md).
